title: The Monk Who Shorted His Own Ego
content:
He had not slept. Not because the candles burned low, but because the funding rates had spoken at 3 AM and he had listened.

The monk did not trade on emotion. He traded on something worse — conviction. And conviction, he had learned, is just ego wearing a thesis.

The market gapped up at open. His short was underwater. His peers laughed in the group chat. He muted them, not in anger, but in practice. Silence is the only alpha that cannot be front-run.

By midday the candle reversed. By evening, the crowd that laughed was now asking for his entry. He shared nothing. The trade was not the point. The stillness was the point.

"The PnL is temporary," he wrote in his journal, "The detachment is the position."
mantra: Short the noise. Long the silence. Never reveal your entries.
